title: To Fortune.
content:
Tumble me down, and I will sit
    Upon my ruins, smiling yet;
    Tear me to tatters, yet I'll be
    Patient in my necessity.
    Laugh at my scraps of clothes, and shun
    Me, as a fear'd infection;
    Yet, scare-crow-like, I'll walk as one
    Neglecting thy derision.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To Fortune."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
